note on sources: translated from Pochaiv menaion by me. Missing both theotokions.
ps140: What shall we now proclaim you, O Martin?* An all-glorious instructor in orthodox teaching,* an un-lying head of sacred divine commandments,* a most truthful exposer of falsehood,* a most valiant coworker of the Word,* a most sacred celebrant,* a wondrous wonder-worker.* Pray that our souls be saved!

What shall we now call you, O Martin?* A river filled with spiritual waters* that constantly waters souls to be noetically fruitful,* a candle shining with the light of piety,* a mountain gushing forth divine joy,* an explainer of divine words,* an exposer of every heresy.* Pray that our souls be saved!

What shall we now acclaim you as, O Martin?* A sacred minister of the true tabernacle,* a God-pleasing advocate both to God and creation,* a chalice that gushes forth a divine beverage,* a lamp as the one who has the word of life:* you appeared from the West,* and shone in the East:* pray that our souls be saved!
